title: Recovery Soup
intro:
Recovery Soup

Across food cultures, restorative soups appear after periods of excess, not as punishment but as recalibration. Legumes, greens, aromatics, and acidity are repeatedly used to support digestion, replenish minerals, and restore balance. This soup is designed for moments when indulgence has already happened and the goal is not restraint, but recovery.

White bean and lentil soups with greens appear throughout Mediterranean and European home cooking, often following holidays or feast days. Italian bean-and-greens soups, French lentil potage, and Iberian legume stews all rely on the same principles: fiber for digestion, greens for bitterness and mineral content, and long simmering for cohesion and comfort. This version modernizes the tradition with layered umami and smoke while remaining plant-forward and cholesterol-free.

ingredientGroups.title: Legume Core
items: 1 cup dried brown or green lentils

ingredientGroups.title: Greens
items: 3 cups kale, stems removed and cut into short ribbons

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Beans
items: Place the cannellini beans in a bowl and cover with several inches of water. Soak 8–12 hours. Drain and rinse before using.

stepGroups.title: Build the Aromatic Base
items: Heat the olive oil in a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, carrots, celery, and garlic. Season with salt and black pepper. Cook 8–10 minutes until softened, lightly golden.
Add the smashed garlic and cook 1–2 minutes until fragrant.

stepGroups.title: Build the Flavor Backbone
items: Add the tomato paste, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, chili flakes, and black pepper.
Cook 2–3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the tomato paste darkens slightly and the spices bloom.

stepGroups.title: Add the Legumes
items: Rinse the cannellini beans and lentils.
Add them directly to the pot along with the bay leaf.

stepGroups.title: Build the Broth
items: Add the chicken stock, water, red miso, and both Better Than Bouillon bases.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Cook 60–90 minutes, until the beans are fully tender and the broth becomes thick and cohesive.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Greens
items: Remove the tough stems from the kale. Stack the leaves, roll into a loose bundle, slice into ribbons, then cut once or twice crosswise to create short rustic strips about 1–1½ inches long.
Place the kale in a bowl with a small pinch of kosher salt and massage gently for about 30 seconds to soften the leaves slightly.

stepGroups.title: Add the Greens
items: Stir in the kale or Swiss chard and the parsley or cilantro.
Simmer about 10 minutes until the greens soften and integrate into the soup.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Soup
items: For additional body, blend 1–2 cups of the soup until smooth and stir it back into the pot.
Add the lemon juice and drizzle lightly with extra-virgin olive oil.
